title: Harlequin FinServe Research Portal
preview:
Account summary: Harlequin is an institutional research group spun out of a mid-size wealth manager. They operate a consolidated knowledge portal used by 1,200 research analysts and downstream quant teams. Primary goal: replace brittle keyword search with vector-backed RAG + reranking to surface internal research, analyst notes, and filings. Strong preference for dedicated GPU pools (predictable latency) and customer-managed KMS.

Key quote on call: "We need sub-100ms median query time for analyst workflows and hard guarantees on residence of EU client data" — Head of Research.

What they care about: indexing scale (currently ~18M docs including slide decks, SEC filings, internal notes), cost per query (target ~$0.002–0.01/query depending on rerank depth), p50/p95 latency targets (p50 < 60ms for vector retrieval + rerank, p95 < 300ms), and tight SLAs for nightly index refreshes. They expect reranking to be a small contextual LM call across top-k=50 results with batching.

Integration notes: Will require SAML SSO, SCIM provisioning, KMS integration with customer HSM (Gemalto/HSM vendor TBD), and audit log export to their SIEM. Data residency: EU-only indices for EEA client documents; US indices for NA docs. They want cross-region routing policies for global analysts but strict storage residency.

Procurement: procurement has asked for a 12–18 month committed Dedicated plan, with a staged POC on a single reserved pool in prod-like VPC. They requested a security review questionnaire (sent 2026-02-12) and a short KMS onboarding runbook.

Sales notes: Good fit for Redwood Dedicated + Optimize. Will ask for a quantized model recommendation for reranker (FP16/4-bit trade-offs) and a sizing spreadsheet for expected QPS peaks (baseline 120 QPS search, peak 450 QPS during market open), plus a fallback policy to hosted smaller models for DR.

Account summary: Harlequin is an institutional research group spun out of a mid-size wealth manager. They operate a consolidated knowledge portal used by 1,200 research analysts and downstream quant teams. Primary goal: replace brittle keyword search with vector-backed RAG + reranking to surface internal research, analyst notes, and filings. Strong preference for dedicated GPU pools (predictable latency) and customer-managed KMS.

Key quote on call: "We need sub-100ms median query time for analyst workflows and hard guarantees on residence of EU client data" — Head of Research.

What they care about: indexing scale (currently ~18M docs including slide decks, SEC filings, internal notes), cost per query (target ~$0.002–0.01/query depending on rerank depth), p50/p95 latency targets (p50 < 60ms for vector retrieval + rerank, p95 < 300ms), and tight SLAs for nightly index refreshes. They expect reranking to be a small contextual LM call across top-k=50 results with batching.

Integration notes: Will require SAML SSO, SCIM provisioning, KMS integration with customer HSM (Gemalto/HSM vendor TBD), and audit log export to their SIEM. Data residency: EU-only indices for EEA client documents; US indices for NA docs. They want cross-region routing policies for global analysts but strict storage residency.

Procurement: procurement has asked for a 12–18 month committed Dedicated plan, with a staged POC on a single reserved pool in prod-like VPC. They requested a security review questionnaire (sent 2026-02-12) and a short KMS onboarding runbook.

Sales notes: Good fit for Redwood Dedicated + Optimize. Will ask for a quantized model recommendation for reranker (FP16/4-bit trade-offs) and a sizing spreadsheet for expected QPS peaks (baseline 120 QPS search, peak 450 QPS during market open), plus a fallback policy to hosted smaller models for DR.

title: Northwind Robotics
preview: Customer to validate streaming stability via eu-west with proxy buffering disabled; Redwood to share best-practice streaming client settings
text: Customer to validate streaming stability via eu-west with proxy buffering disabled; Redwood to share best-practice streaming client settings

title: Northstar Support Platform
preview: Northstar is evaluating Redwood to power an LLM-based support agent assist + summarization feature. Key decision criteria: (1) onboarding speed and clarity (TTFS/TTFP), (2) production-safe defaults for streaming + retries/timeouts, (3) predictable cost controls (quotas/budgets + usage analytics), (4) model pinning + fallback routing, and (5) security posture (SSO/SAML, audit logs, US data residency, retention controls, KMS). They prefer VPC deployment but may start on Hosted API if security review can proceed in parallel.
text: Northstar is evaluating Redwood to power an LLM-based support agent assist + summarization feature. Key decision criteria: (1) onboarding speed and clarity (TTFS/TTFP), (2) production-safe defaults for streaming + retries/timeouts, (3) predictable cost controls (quotas/budgets + usage analytics), (4) model pinning + fallback routing, and (5) security posture (SSO/SAML, audit logs, US data residency, retention controls, KMS). They prefer VPC deployment but may start on Hosted API if security review can proceed in parallel.

title: BluePeak AI
preview: Workload is real-time customer support agent (chat + summarization) with streaming responses. Early production target: ~25 RPS bursts, P95 latency goal < 1.5s for short replies, cost-sensitive but willing to pay for reliability. Wants clear controls for quotas/budgets, and guidance for safe client behavior (timeouts/retries, streaming reconnect). Preference for sticking to a single pinned model version for the first month; interested in fallback policy once traffic scales.

title: Lattice Search
preview:
Account summary
- Enterprise doc-search team building an internal + customer-facing knowledge search experience across tickets, docs, and Slack/Confluence exports.
- Strong preference for a "production launch checklist" they can use as an internal approval artifact (security + platform teams want a clear list of go-live requirements).

Workload / technical fit
- Primary workload: embeddings + reranking for document retrieval; secondary: summarization of retrieved passages.
- Latency: wants p95 < 500ms for rerank on typical query traffic; embeddings batch jobs can be async.
- Model posture: open to Redwood catalog defaults, but wants ability to pin model version and have a defined rollback/fallback policy.
- Observability: wants token/cost breakdown + request tracing hooks; asked specifically how to audit "who changed model settings" and "when".

Security + compliance (main theme)
- Customer quote (paraphrased): "We can’t get this through internal review without a clear go-live checklist and evidence we followed it."
- Requirements called out repeatedly:
  - SSO/SAML for Console access and role-based access controls.
  - Audit logging (admin actions + model/config changes).
  - KMS integration for keys/secrets at rest (customer-managed keys preferred; at minimum, clear encryption statement).
  - Data retention controls for logs/telemetry; hard requirement to avoid PII in onboarding/progress events.
  - Data residency: mixed — US default ok, but EU subsidiaries require EU region storage and clarity on where telemetry is stored.

Procurement / timeline
- Customer-side procurement process is active; target to sign by end of April pending security questionnaire + DPA redlines.
- Gating items: security team questionnaire, legal DPA review, and platform team sign-off on VPC architecture.

Onboarding checklist / product feedback
- They responded well to the Console checklist concept, but want:
  - Exportable proof (screenshot/PDF or API) of checklist completion for internal change management.
  - Clear definitions of "First success" vs "First production traffic" and what events count.
  - Explicit steps around retries/timeouts, 429 handling, streaming disconnect behavior (even if their first phase is not streaming).
  - A step for quotas/budgets and alerting (they’ve been burned by surprise usage spikes).

Open questions
- Confirm preferred deployment path: Dedicated in Redwood-managed VPC vs Redwood Private in their VPC (leaning Private).
- Confirm regions required on day 1 (US only vs US+EU).
- Validate whether any documents include regulated data categories (HIPAA/PCI) — customer said "no" but needs verification.

Internal owners / handoffs
- Ben Carter (owner): coordinate procurement timeline + stakeholder map.
- Brian Murphy (SE): drive technical validation (routing/fallback, observability hooks, audit logging expectations).
- Dev Patel (Support/CS): review what support needs to interpret checklist state during pilot + escalation paths.

Next actions
- Send security packet: onboarding telemetry data review summary, event schema notes (PII avoidance), retention defaults + knobs.
- Provide VPC/private deployment architecture overview + responsibilities (shared responsibility model).
- Share draft "First Production Launch" checklist template and confirm which steps are mandatory for their internal approval.
